title: ChEMBL drug-normalization CC-BY-SA overlay
context: ChEMBL is the canonical drug identifier source used for normalization in this project, but ChEMBL data is CC-BY-SA-3.0 (share-alike), which means any derivative must also be CC-BY-SA-3.0 and must be isolated from the CC0 core. A segregated ChEMBL overlay provides richer drug labels, synonyms, and chemical identifiers without contaminating the CC0 core or CC-BY overlays. Share-alike isolation is mandatory.
objective: Produce a CC-BY-SA-3.0 ChEMBL drug-normalization overlay providing drug names, synonyms, and chemical identifiers for drug entities in the CC0 core, packaged as a fully isolated share-alike overlay with its own license manifest.
acceptanceCriteria: ChEMBL drug names, synonyms, and chemical identifiers are extracted for drug entities present in the CC0 core.
Overlay is packaged in complete isolation from the CC0 core and CC-BY overlays, with a CC-BY-SA-3.0 license manifest.
Every overlay entry carries provenance: source=ChEMBL, version, license=CC-BY-SA-3.0.
ChEMBL's allowlist entry is approved for this overlay before ingestion.
A data README explicitly warns about the CC-BY-SA-3.0 share-alike obligation on derivatives.
output: data/overlays/chembl/ directory with the CC-BY-SA-3.0 ChEMBL drug overlay dataset and license manifest, committed via PR.
